name: HD 209458 b
description: HD 209458 b is a classic hot Jupiter transit target. Its host star is bright and the transit depth is large enough to make it a strong first-choice system for educational TESS transit analysis.

name: WASP-43 b
description: WASP-43 b is a short-period hot Jupiter with frequent transits. Its repeated events make it useful for phase-folded transit exploration with TESS sectors.

name: WASP-18 b
description: WASP-18 b is a bright-host hot Jupiter system with deep, frequent transits. It is a practical target for a simple aperture-plus-comparison-star workflow.

name: HAT-P-7 b
description: HAT-P-7 b is a bright Kepler-field hot Jupiter with a short period and multiple TESS sectors, making it useful for a compact transit workflow with repeated events.

name: WASP-121 b
description: WASP-121 b is a bright ultra-hot Jupiter with deep, frequent transits. It is a strong target when you want clear transit structure within a short time span.

name: GJ 436 b
description: GJ 436 b is a well-known warm Neptune around a relatively bright host. It broadens the sample beyond hot Jupiters while staying accessible to the same TESS workflow.

name: WASP-19 b
description: WASP-19 b has an extremely short period, so transits repeat quickly within a sector. It is useful when you want a dense sequence of transit events to inspect.

name: WASP-4 b
description: WASP-4 b is a classic short-period hot Jupiter and a practical comparison target alongside WASP-18 b for southern-sector transit exploration.

name: Qatar-1 b
description: Qatar-1 b is a compact hot Jupiter system with multiple northern TESS sectors. It adds another repeat-transit target with a moderately bright host star.
